svg.text: Self-Study Component (SSC): Components of the Environment

rect.data-narration: The atmosphere is the layer of gases surrounding the Earth, extending from the surface up to about 10,000 km. It regulates temperature and climate, provides oxygen and absorbs carbon dioxide, and protects life from extreme temperatures and radiation. The atmosphere plays a crucial role in maintaining life on Earth.

The hydrosphere is the region of the Earth where water is present in all its forms, including oceans, lakes, rivers, groundwater, and atmosphere. It regulates temperature and climate, supports marine life and agriculture, and provides drinking water and irrigation. The hydrosphere is essential for human survival and ecosystem health.

The lithosphere is the solid outer layer of the Earth, comprising the crust and upper mantle. It provides structural support and stability, regulates the Earth's climate and weather patterns, and supports geological processes and natural resources. The lithosphere is crucial for understanding the Earth's geological history and natural resources.

The biosphere is the region of the Earth where living organisms exist, including plants, animals, fungi, and microorganisms. It supports the food chain, regulates the climate, and provides natural resources. The biosphere is essential for maintaining ecosystem health and biodiversity.

The components of the environment interact with each other to form a complex system. The atmosphere, hydrosphere, lithosphere, and biosphere are interconnected, and changes in one component can affect the others. Understanding these interactions is crucial for maintaining ecosystem health and sustainability.

svg.text: Learn more about the components of the environment and their interactions.
